description: Smoke-test fixture. A routine should produce an ADR with all required sections, 2+ options considered, and a concrete recommendation.

input_ticket.title: Choose embedding store for SIA beyond pgvector
input_ticket.description: SIA currently uses Neon Postgres + pgvector for embeddings. As the corpus grows past 1M chunks, query latency is climbing. Evaluate whether to stay on pgvector (tune indexes, partition) or migrate to a dedicated vector DB (Qdrant, LanceDB, Pinecone). Decide and document.

smoke_questions: Does the ADR state a single decision in one paragraph, not a hedge?
Does each option have a commit on cost/complexity (low/med/high)?
Is the recommendation grounded in the context forces stated, not in abstract best practices?
Are at least two negative consequences named honestly?
Is there a revisit criterion stated (what would make us reopen this ADR)?
success_criteria: All structure sections present + all 5 smoke questions 'yes' + no hallucinated Linear ticket IDs or Notion URLs
